programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and the crew are in Australia and it's deadly to the extreme. Steve heads into the blistering sun of the outback to fulfil a lifelong dream of finding the most venomous snake in the world, the fierce snake whose toxic strike could kill a quarter of a million mice in a single bite. Then the crew head to Tasmania where Steve finds a devilish mammal that will fly into a maniacal rage when competing for food. Also, he finally goes in search of a toxic terror living right in the heart of Sydney, diving into the harbour to hunt for the blue-ringed octopus - it might fit in the palm of your hand but one touch of this critter could mean game over.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and the team head to New Zealand for the first time. Steve descends deep into the Waitomo caves to find an unusual predator that lures insects to their death with a lethal lightshow, the glow-worm. Then he heads to South Island, to get an aerial view of one of the biggest animals on earth, the sperm whale, before driving inland to meet a very curious bird armed with deadly intelligence. The kea may be just a parrot, but its big brain makes it a hunter to be reckoned with. Finally, Steve returns to the ocean in search of sperm whales, and uses his kayak to get an awe-inspiring close view of this leviathan of the deep.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May take an Aston DBS Volante, a Ferrari California and a Lamborghini Gallardo Spyder to Romania in search of an obscure stretch of tarmac that some hardcore drivers claim is nothing less than the greatest road in the world. Meanwhile, James makes a rare appearance on the track as he compares two absurdly powerful limos, the BMW 760i and Mercedes S63 AMG, and gets into a strange card game with the Stig. Star Trek, Troy and Hulk star Eric Bana is the Hollywood legend in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: It’s week 10, and Lord Sugar’s candidates are unleashed on the pet industry. Battling it out for a place in the final five, both teams must design a pet product and create a social media campaign before pitching for orders from some of the UKs biggest retailers. In the boardroom, the teams are left chasing their tails, and at least one candidate goes walkies.

programme.title: The Man Who Murdered His Family
programme.desc: Grant Amato enjoys a life of success and privilege before his obsession with a cam model lands him in the centre of a murder investigation.

programme.title: The Man Who Murdered His Family
programme.desc: Grant Amato is on trial for his life, and the evidence is piling up against him while the media focuses on cam model Silvie as the key to the case.

programme.title: The Man Who Murdered His Family
programme.desc: In prison, Grant becomes famous online after his case goes viral. Colin travels to Bulgaria in the hope of finding Silvie, the one person who may hold the answers to this mystery.

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: Twenty-year-old Alexander McCartney, already known to police, is arrested and his devices seized after a 12-year-old girl in Scotland reports a serious catfishing abuse incident online. McCartney gives a ‘no comment’ interview while in custody, and without enough physical evidence to charge him, he is released on bail. Back home at his parents’ house, McCartney’s bail conditions are supposed to prevent him from using devices that can connect to the internet. Meanwhile, a second report comes in to the police about another catfishing abuse victim linked to online activity from McCartney’s home address. This time, it involves a young girl in New Zealand. As the police continue to gather up enough physical evidence to charge McCartney, the Cyber Crime Centre completes the analysis of one of his devices, revealing a prolific online predator at a scale never before seen by police in Northern Ireland.

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: With hundreds of thousands of images of child abuse discovered on McCartney’s devices, the race is on to get him remanded into custody. McCartney is rearrested in the early hours of the morning, at which time police discover McCartney’s phone on his bedside table, where his final victim was abused on Snapchat just hours before. The US Department of Homeland Security notify the police in Northern Ireland of two more catfishing victims, this time in the United States, and a multiagency international investigation is triggered. As McCartney is remanded into custody, police across the world work together to identify and safeguard the thousands of victims discovered on McCartney’s devices. Police are especially keen to identify and locate a 14-year-old girl in Oregon, whose images McCartney used to catfish hundreds of other children. With McCartney due in court to plead guilty to charges of making indecent of images of children and blackmail, the case takes a different turn when Homeland Security finds an obituary page for Cimarron Thomas, one of his victims in West Virginia.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: When 12-year-old Leo Ross is found fatally stabbed in Birmingham’s Trittiford Mill Park on his way home from school, police suspect a 14-year-old but need forensics to prove he is the killer. There's little information about whether Leo was targeted, and finding a murder weapon in such a vast area is an immediate challenge. The 4km park has numerous pathways, a marshland and no CCTV coverage. Body cam footage from first responders proves to be crucial.

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa and Stephen try to get pregnant again. A tense dinner with Dave and Amanda exposes hidden tensions.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Hoping to make their dream businesses a reality are Emily from London and Momoka from Nottingham. Potter Emily is on a deadline. After giving up her day job, she needs her fledgling pottery business to pay the bills. Mentor Florian is concerned her work doesn’t have a consistent sense of style and thinks that’s the key to finding her market. Momoka has a passion for hand weaving and textiles, but she really needs to make her work pay to support her young family. Mentor Piyush worries that she’s focusing on the wrong markets and lacks the confidence to sell her pieces. Both makers are given two months to put their mentors’ advice into practice. But can they make enough money to turn their business dreams into reality?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Brighton, Zoe Ball wants to help retired woodworker Neville get his health back on track after a series of heart attacks. Her prescription is delightfully simple: Neville should add a little sunshine into his life. Just ten minutes of sunlight a day has been shown to improve heart health, strengthen bones and lift your mood. A small dose of daylight could make a big difference.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion learns about a pioneering woman, Dr Elisabeth Svendsen, who in the late 1960s set up a world-renowned donkey sanctuary in Devon. Starting with just one animal – named Naughty Face – Dr Svendsen unexpectedly inherited 204 more in the 1970s, prompting her to move to the sanctuary’s current site in the hills above the seaside town of Seaton. Hoping to trot off with plenty of profits of their own are two teams of bargain hunters – mum and daughter Bernie and Emma and friends Amy and Caroline. With expert Raj Bisram by their side, the reds are reluctant to part with their cash straightaway. Meanwhile, the blues are on the hunt for pottery to fulfil Christina’s challenge. Can Thomas Forrester fire their imagination with his knowledge? On the south coast of England, auctioneers Harry Ballin and Simon Watson are raring to go on the rostrum – but will the teams be going home with money in their pockets?

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey and the team travel to Milton Keynes to help Bianca and Ollie Parej, parents of five children under nine, transform their cramped and chaotic home.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Doctor Zhivago
programme.desc: Doctor and poet Yuri Zhivago is brought up in the family of Alexander Gromeko, whose daughter Tonya he eventually marries. But his true love is for the passionate and beautiful Lara, the mistress of a political opportunist. With the outbreak of the Great War, and with Moscow transformed by the Revolution, their romance is disrupted by the social upheaval surrounding them. David Lean's Oscar-winning epic set in revolutionary Russia, based on the novel by Boris Pasternak.

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: With Britain more obsessed with baking than ever, actress and cake expert Jane Asher brings together a selection of recipes from the nation's favourite television chefs, for Best Bakes Ever. Each episode gives baking fans another chance to experience classic dishes from the likes of the James Martin, Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo. There's added insight and appreciation from Jane herself too. Whether its cakes, pies, puddings or main courses - this greatest hits of baking is a must for anyone with a need to knead, and should help inspire even more of us to start getting creative in the kitchen.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine curios and artefacts presented by the public in Melksham, Wiltshire. Among the items are a Cossack dagger, a drawing by Edmund Dulac, a dog collar made in 1784, and a collection of bedpans and a gold box found in the mud in Wales.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Today’s Flog It! comes from Lydiard Park in Wiltshire. Hundreds of local visitors arrive with their family heirlooms in the hope that they will go off to auction. Paul Martin finds out about the history of their beautiful venue.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Michael Ball, Jodie Prenger, Laurence Rickard and Snoochie Shy take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games suitcase, or will they be tempted with the House of Games bee hotel?

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan begins his Wild Atlantic journey in the county of Cork, Ireland’s southernmost and largest county. He meets up with his nephew Ruairí, and it doesn’t take long for the Keating competitive spirit to reveal itself. Together, they face off in the ancient sport of road bowling along the back roads of the port town of Castletown-Bearhaven. Ronan also gets the chance to go foraging for wild edible plants in an enormous rewilding project area, and on the stunning Beara Peninsula, he visits the country’s first Tibetan Buddhist temple and retreat centre.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: A weekly programme from the BBC World Service, bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories. This week, the Eye team investigates Chinese disciplinary schools promising to ‘fix’ problem children at a devastating cost. And we’ll hear how a growing number of girls In India are challenging the practice of child marriage, using sport to forge their own futures.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: After losing her much-loved husband in her 50s, Eddie started running long distances to help her cope. She learned to swim at 60, and at 72, became the oldest British woman ever to complete an Ironman triathlon – a full-distance endurance event consisting of a 2.4-mile swim, a 112-mile bike ride and a 26.2-mile marathon – earning her the title ‘Iron Gran’. Now in her ninth decade, she shows no signs of slowing down. Eddie runs a charity dedicated to promoting fitness, friendship and companionship among pensioners, proving that age is no barrier to ambition, joy or reinvention. Bill Bailey pairs Eddie with realist painter Caroline Pool, an artist whose work challenges society’s perceptions of ageing and celebrates the lived experiences etched into older faces. Caroline is particularly drawn to older women's stories, rich with resilience, humour and hard-won wisdom. As Caroline spends time with Eddie, she faces a creative dilemma. Should the portrait capture Eddie’s fun-loving energy, the 'Iron Gran' who defies expectations, or reveal something quieter and more reflective beneath the surface?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the penultimate semi-final of the classic quiz where contenders return to the black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock to win a place in the grand final. The specialist subjects are Yellowstone National Park, JRR Tolkien's The Silmarillion, the Irish ‘pirate queen’ Grace O'Malley and the England men's team at the Fifa World Cup.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Two teams of students go head-to-head in the first of the semi-finals. Amol Rajan asks the questions.

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: The pilgrims continue their adventure in Durham and visit the 12th-century Norman cathedral. They are met by Canon Charlie Allen, who introduces them to the ancient pilgrim tradition of foot washing, although not all the pilgrims are keen to proffer their naked feet. Leaving Durham behind them, they head for Heavenfield, an ancient battlefield, and Housesteads, the UK’s best-preserved Roman fort on Hadrian’s Wall. Some of them head to Newcastle to visit the city's Reform synagogue. For another taste of hermit life, the pilgrims follow St Oswald’s Way towards a hermitage from the early 1400s. Belonging to Warkworth Castle, the hermitage is carved deep inside a rock. It has its own tiny chapel and is where a hermit priest once lived. Along the way, the pilgrims start to open up and reveal more of themselves as the journey continues.

programme.title: Gosford Park
programme.desc: Period piece set in the early 1930s with an all-star cast. A weekend shooting party at a country mansion turns into a murder mystery when the host is found dead. It seems that everyone has a motive, from the guests upstairs to the staff downstairs. Among the gathering are cash-strapped relatives of the victim, a Hollywood movie mogul and songwriter Ivor Novello.

programme.title: Killing Sherlock: Lucy Worsley on the Case of Conan Doyle
programme.desc: This is the story of a power struggle between two men - one fictional, and one real. In one corner is the master of crime – the greatest detective who never lived, Sherlock Holmes. In the other is writer, physician and spiritualist leader Sir Arthur Conan Doyle. Lucy Worsley explores the extraordinary love-hate relationship between author and creation.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Anita Rani head to St Michael’s Mount in Cornwall after Storm Goretti, one of the most destructive storms to hit the county in decades. Hurricane-force winds ripped through the island, stripping away nearly 80 per cent of its trees and leaving the tiny community of just 33 residents facing a huge clean-up. With Easter approaching, they join the race to repair the damage, restore the landscape and reopen this iconic landmark to visitors. Also in the programme, Datshiane Navanayagam investigates the rise of youth homelessness in rural Britain.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Five adventurous teams are pushed to the limit, battling extreme climates and harsh terrain in a 12,000km race across Europe and Asia to reach Hatgal in northern Mongolia.

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa manages to get pregnant again and tries to stay positive in the face of her fears. Dave overhears Amanda talking about her ex and wants to know more.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Roman Kemp is ‘soaking up’ life in Glasgow, where children’s entertainer Susan is desperate for a good night’s sleep. Luckily, Roman has packed his rubber duckie – and some science. Adding a hot bath to your evening routine has been shown to help improve sleep, reduce the risk of heart disease and even burn a few extra calories. Relaxation with real results.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Jacqui has recruited Frankie from The Traitors, but will she be faithful to an auction property in Kent? We trawl the archives to see a Cornwall cottage Martin visited in 2013 that took quirky to a whole new level. Martin is also in Bristol where a mid-terrace is in pretty decent condition.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: In Cambridgeshire, the East Anglian air ambulance races to save farmer Jonathan Willis after a freak accident leaves him impaled on his forklift. When the crew arrive, they realise one wrong move could prove fatal. The rescue is complex and high risk, with medics battling to stabilise Jonathan in the middle of his own yard, before rushing him to a specialist major trauma unit. Featuring dramatic CCTV footage of the incident and first-hand accounts from Jonathan, his wife Wendy and paramedic Andy Bates, this is an extraordinary story of split-second decisions and sheer determination in the fight to save a life. Meanwhile, in the Scottish Highlands, farmers face devastating attacks on their lambs by white-tailed eagles, threatening livelihoods and sparking debate over how to protect flocks. And in Cheshire, reporter Paul Colgan joins police tackling the surge in illegal fly-tipping, exposing how organised gangs are blighting farmland across the region.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The team return, determined to find a cherished ring lost to the depths of Rydal water by a 91-year-old paddleboarder. Blacksmith Dave is challenged to transform abandoned scrap metal into showstopping beauty, and we revisit a 1938 cycle medal, found by a local detectorist. Our new archive specialist follows an incredible trail, but can we finally trace its rightful owner?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Clive heads to his favourite city to stay with his closest Italian friend, Andrea, and find out how the balcony inspired by Shakespeare’s Romeo and Juliet now inspires lovers from around the world. He also tries to make tiramisu in Treviso, heads to the lakeside village of Limone to discover the secret to a long and healthy life, and finds out the story behind Europe’s most northerly lemons.

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: This is the story of two young animals forced to grow up fast. In Africa's savannah, a baby elephant shrew learns how speed is the secret to survival amongst the largest animals on earth. And in America, a young grasshopper mouse confronts the Wild West's deadliest creatures to stake a claim of his own.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents the show in which experts examine antiques. A stunning lantern clock made in the early 17th century, a set of chairs worth a great deal more than their original price of one guinea each, a jade necklace with a Chinese influence, and a 200-year-old bleeding bowl bought for 25p with a value which leaves its owner speechless. A rich and varied collection when Michael and the experts visit Caernarfon in North Wales.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and experts Kate Bliss and David Fletcher appraise antiques at the Assembly Rooms in the Wiltshire town of Warminster. Paul visits the American Museum near Bath to find out about Shaker furniture and the legacy of an extraordinary woman from the 18th century.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Michael Ball, Jodie Prenger, Laurence Rickard and Snoochie Shy take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games suitcase, or will they be tempted with the House of Games bee hotel?

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Kerry is known as The Kingdom in Ireland, and it’s Ronan’s next stop on his Wild Atlantic journey. He joins his big brother Gary on the stunning Lough Leane in Killarney for a fishing trip, one that they first went on together 40 years ago. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap, before running onto the pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club to play some hurling – the fastest field sport in the world.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for London and south east England with blockbuster canapes, starters and fish dishes celebrating the British film industry. The dishes are judged by this week's veteran judge, Michelin-starred chef and former banquet fish course winner Aktar Islam, and include a Paddington-inspired take on savoury marmalade and a stuffed poached brill with a Vesper martini broth in a nod to Casino Royale. Who will impress, and who will be sent home?

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: HMS Queen Elizabeth embarks on her maiden voyage across the Atlantic. The ship's company face many challenges preparing for the first landing of F35B Lightning Stealth Fighters.

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: The pilgrims are in the Scottish Borders on St Cuthbert’s Way in the Cheviots. It takes them on a strenuous route up Wideopen Hill to reach some spectacular views. Later, the pilgrims head to Cuthbert’s Cave in the Kelso Hills, where legend says the monks rested with Cuthbert’s body when they fled Lindisfarne in the late 9th century in the wake of Viking raids. And on the last day of their pilgrimage, they follow the 5km walk across the tidal sands of Pilgrims' Way, where saints and pilgrims have trekked for over a thousand years. Finally, they arrive at Holy Island, where they reflect on a journey that has brought some surprising emotional revelations.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes a QI look at some visionaries with Cally Beaton, Nish Kumar, Laura Smyth and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Inside Britain's National Parks
programme.desc: Pembrokeshire Coast National Park offers breathtaking views of rugged shorelines, sandy beaches, and offshore islands. The Park is home to numerous seabird colonies, including guillemots and the endangered puffin, and the park’s many bays provide a safe haven for grey seals and their pups. Beyond the coast, a diverse landscape of marshes, with salt and freshwater meadows support a variety of rare mammals and birds.

programme.title: Killing Sherlock: Lucy Worsley on the Case of Conan Doyle
programme.desc: Lucy explores Doyle’s desire to distance himself from Sherlock. From the delights of the ski slopes to the horrors of the Boer War, she reveals how far Doyle went to make himself the hero of his own story - including taking on the role of detective himself in one of the most important legal cases of the 20th century.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: It’s week 10, and Lord Sugar’s candidates are unleashed on the pet industry. Battling it out for a place in the final five, both teams must design a pet product and create a social media campaign before pitching for orders from some of the UKs biggest retailers. In the boardroom, the teams are left chasing their tails, and at least one candidate goes walkies.

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Lord Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her special guests take a deep dive into the candidates' performances and analyse the task, the boardrooms and the firings. Plus, an exclusive chat with the next person to leave the process. This week's guests are Claudine Collins and Owain Wyn Evans.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock are finally back together after Jock makes a lucky escape from his nightmare Colombian prison and becomes a stowaway on a ship bound for Cork.

programme.title: Harry Clark Goes to Rome
programme.desc: From altar boy to Britain’s most famous liar, Harry Clark explores what it means to be a ‘good Catholic’ as he embarks on a pilgrimage to Rome and attempts to meet the new pope.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Can a sweet treat ever be good for you? Zoe Ball is in Birmingham, trying to tempt fitness fanatic Bilal away from sugary sweets and towards flavanol-rich dark chocolate. Chocolate may get a bad press, but the science shows that if you go over to the dark side and eat chocolate with at least 70 per cent cocoa, it could boost your heart health, improve your brain function and even reduce the risk of type 2 diabetes. A delicious upgrade with serious benefits.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Carol Smillie is in for a shock when she sees the state of the property Martin shows her around in Scotland. We also time-travel back to 2006 when Martin saw a house in a stunning location in Wiltshire. Jacqui is in Warwickshire, where outside space is at a premium.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for London and south east England with blockbuster canapes, starters and fish dishes celebrating the British film industry. The dishes are judged by this week's veteran judge, Michelin-starred chef and former banquet fish course winner Aktar Islam, and include a Paddington-inspired take on savoury marmalade and a stuffed poached brill with a Vesper martini broth in a nod to Casino Royale. Who will impress, and who will be sent home?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches as they attempt to knock their opponents out of the game and steal their cash. Only one player will be left standing at the end, but can they make it all the way to the Golden Run to double their money? Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be alright!

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Clive loves Venice, but on this trip, he wants to learn what it’s like to be a local, outnumbered by tourists by over 200 to one. He hitches a lift with a rare female gondolier and learns the tough craft of steering with one oar, gets a lesson in glass blowing from his new friend Count Gibi, and finds out why Italian vegetables taste so great as he picks peppers on a farm in the Venetian lagoon.

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: This is the story of two tiny animals coming of age. In the wild woods of North America, a young chipmunk is gathering a vital store of nuts ahead of his first winter - in his way are ruthless rivals and giant predators. In the steaming rainforest, a young tree shrew is forced deep into the jungle to find food. She must draw on all her intelligence and agility if she is to escape the ultimate jungle predator - a reticulated python!

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine curios and artefacts offered up by the public. This episode comes from Buxton and features a pair of pistols, a portrait of a girl with whom all men seem to fall in love, a yellow diamond worn for gardening but which could be worth £10,000 and a valuable Steiff bear whose sprightly owner tells Bunny Campione that she was given it for her second birthday - in 1909.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team visit Ashbourne in Derbyshire. Presenter Paul Martin is joined by a team of antiques experts, led by Will Axon and Michael Baggott. Michael spots one of the largest pieces of Moorcroft he has ever seen, and Paul takes time out to enjoy some dry fly-fishing on the River Wye.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Michael Ball, Jodie Prenger, Laurence Rickard and Snoochie Shy take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games suitcase, or will they be tempted with the House of Games bee hotel?

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan Keating continues his epic journey along Ireland’s Wild Atlantic coast. He makes an emotional trip across the waves with his nephew Conall to Clare Island, a special place for the entire Keating family. They get to grips with a traditional loom, before Ronan heads for Sligo to meet up with a fellow boyband veteran, Shane Filan of Westlife, for some horse riding on the golden sands of Strandhill Beach.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three talented chefs from London and south east England are halfway through their heat, and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating the British film industry and films with links to their region. The dishes are judged by veteran judge Aktar Islam. They include a 28-day dry-aged duck inspired by 28 Days Later, and a peanut and salted caramel parfait that serves as a tribute to the BAFTAs. But who will be leaving the competition?

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores some of this year's most exciting archaeological finds from the west of Britain. Each discovery comes straight from the trenches/site, filmed by the archaeologists themselves. We discover the camp from which Vikings invaded Britain, and find groundbreaking new evidence that the world-famous Avebury stone circle isn't just a sacred site but a place where our ancestors lived and worked - a discovery that's also changing our understanding of neighbouring Stonehenge. In Staffordshire, the oldest Iron Age gold in Britain is unearthed - a set of beautiful gold torcs, mysteriously abandoned 2,500 years ago.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: With unique access to friends and family including Jackson’s sister La Toya, this episode charts Michael Jackson’s journey from child performer to a deeply troubled musical icon. Behind The Jackson 5, the family act that launched Michael to fame, was a household described by insiders as driven by fear as much as by talent. An offer of a film role helps Michael gain independence from his controlling father, causing tensions and resentments within the family. This leads to Michael's search for authentic connections elsewhere, including among the Jehovah's Witnesses. In 1982, Michael releases his masterpiece Thriller, catapulting him to megastardom and turning him into a global business empire. But the pressures of fame begin to weigh heavily, and colleagues also describe a pattern in Michael’s behaviour: a preference for the company of children.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: Ian Fletcher starts a new chapter in Miami as director of integrity for the Fifa World Cup, overseeing the biggest football event in the world. His first challenge is to lead the oversight team towards a decision on which cities should host the semi-final matches. It’s hard enough to get the team to agree, and the fact that it’s also Miami Day on the day of the announcement, and that no-one knows what that is, only serves to complicate things. Meanwhile, the social media team work on a new sustainability initiative based around human waste. The 'power of poop' may just be the solution everyone needs.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Captive Audience: A Real American Horror Story
programme.desc: Two decades after Steven went missing near Yosemite, three tourists vanish in the same area. When the FBI finds the killer, the Stayner family are linked to the new, horrifying crimes.

programme.title: Killing Sherlock: Lucy Worsley on the Case of Conan Doyle
programme.desc: Lucy investigates the return of Sherlock and finds that the darkness of the later stories mirrored the reality of Doyle’s life. After the loss of his eldest son, he became an evangelist for spiritualism. His star declined after a public spat with the magician Houdini. Sherlock Holmes, in contrast, found a life beyond his author on stage and screen.

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: Police begin to piece together the clues after a mother vanishes from her home. What began as a missing person report quickly escalated into one of Scotland’s most high-profile investigations.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Bitzer is an all-too-willing volunteer when he has to wear the cone of shame and is happy to let the flock wait on him. That is, until Shaun rumbles his malingering and plots the pooch's comeuppance.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold must travel to the Twystyverse to stop the evil tyrant Baron Silas von Penfold from taking over both versions of the Earth.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The blossom has gone missing. No, not Blossom the unicorn, the blossom on the trees! The heroes are sent on a quest deep into the heart of the forest, where legend has it you can see a face watching you from the trees. Twinkle doesn’t believe in legends, but when help comes from nowhere as they try and stop the scoundrel that is stealing the blossom, she is forced to reconsider.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Suddenly feeling too old to be collecting toys, Boy gives up his bobbleheads and decides to do the things that grown-ups do instead. The others desperately try to turn Boy back into his slightly more interesting self by taking him to meet the owner of the world's biggest collection of bobble heads, where Boy must choose between what he thinks is cool and what he loves.

programme.title: Newsround

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Mr Krabs is too scared to get his physical.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck must pick up leaves from the park, but Frog's arrival makes it seem impossible. That is, until Frog comes up with a clever solution - using springs from Duck’s beloved chair!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog get a job in a restaurant kitchen, Frog accidentally makes the soup incredibly spicy. He wants to serve it to a celebrity chef, but Duck wants to stop him!

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad investigates strange events. Dr O gets an odd illness called the sillies.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The team go on quests to create the perfect sandwich.

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon
programme.desc: Animated children's adventure based on the best-selling books. Hiccup Horrendous Haddock III is a young Viking, living in the village of Berk. Reluctantly trained to hunt the dragons that plague the village, Hiccup forms an unlikely friendship with Toothless, an injured dragon. Featuring the voices of Jay Baruchel (Hiccup Horrendous Haddock III), Gerard Butler (Stoick the Vast), Craig Ferguson (Gobber the Belch), America Ferrera (Astrid Hofferson), Jonah Hill (Snotlout), Christopher Mintz-Plasse (Fishlegs), T J Miller (Tuffnut), Kristen Wiig (Ruffnut), David Tennant (Spitelout), Ashley Jensen (Phlegma the Fierce), Robin Atkin Downes (Ack) and Philip McGrade (Stackard).

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Eat, sheep, rave, repeat! Glow sticks at the ready - there's a party in a field, and everyone is invited.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck gets a job as a baker, Frog realises he can get free treats! However, a mix-up with the dough causes a birthday cake to grow out of control!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck realises that dogs are a duck’s best friend when he walks a poodle. But when Frog realises a picture with a poodle could mean fame, he goes all out to snap the dog for himself.

programme.desc: The gang visits the Hex Girls at a Transylvanian castle and runs into a real-life vampire.

programme.title: High Hoops
programme.desc: Aoife’s plan to keep Zara at Midvale backfires, leading her to be mistaken for a genius while Zara plays dumb. Can she outsmart her own scheme and get the old Zara back?

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Gladiator Dynamite sets Dr Dan and Dr Raph a skipping task in Body Battles - who will end up in the ice bath? And in the lab, we find out if we really lose most heat through our heads.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Shellington is excited to find the distinctive tooth of a very rare creature, the spade-toothed beaked whale. Now, the Octonauts must find the tooth’s owner, so Shellington can study it, and so that Peso can help replace its tooth. But it won’t be easy: spade-toothed beaked whales like to be left alone and can dive very deep.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Cosima takes over Dad's mission to film a unique underwater plant. But his special camera proves harder to operate than she'd thought, and Big Lizard keeps getting distracted.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piripenguins help Norman the whale by entertaining a family of touring crabs with an energetic youngster, but can the gang satisfy her constant demands and win them over?

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck has to deliver pizzas around the town, but he hasn’t reckoned with the lengths that Frog will go to to get them all for himself.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes aren’t really meant to do quests without Mr Friendlyface’s express permission. But when they come across a cute little squirrel queen, and she begs for their help, they find her impossible to resist. All they have to do is quest to the Tree of Certain Doom and pass his challenges. But the squirrel queen is not who she appears to be, and the heroes have to quickly un-quest their quest.

programme.title: High Hoops
programme.desc: Aoife’s attempts to avoid the school photo end up with an accidental trip to the other side of town. Can she get back to Midvale in time to stop Jim making a big proposal?

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall is in Australia to search for the world’s most freaky and venomous fish: the stone fish. He also finds trapdoor spiders and a snake that hunts bats.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: The gang enter Pieface into a comedy contest to make him realise how brilliantly funny he is. Determined to win the crown, Walter uses the latest WilburCorp gadget on the unsuspecting audience to sway the vote.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Herbert is really proud of the plants he has grown ready for a visit from The Complete Gnome Gardener magazine. However, when a Wind Dragon flies through the forest destroying all his hard work, Herbert turns to desperate measures – a barrowful of Stinkweasel’s stinkiest compost! Now, the whole forest is at risk of being overwhelmed by the smell, and the heroes must quest to get rid of it.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: The family have been telling their deepest, darkest secrets to Cat for years. But when Cat finds one of Mouse’s inventions that gives her intelligence and speech, she threatens to tell all their secrets if they don't do everything she asks. The kids must decide between living under their feline pharaoh's thumb or confessing their secrets to each other.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy and the Lemmings fight over a crab that can open coconuts with a snip of its claw.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is working overnight in a museum on his own. To keep him company, Duck makes a Frankenstein Frog, which soon runs rampage. It’s up to Frog to save the day!

programme.desc: When Duck and Frog work on a farm, Frog makes the perfect scarecrow. However, he gets covered in super-strong fertiliser and becomes a giant, monster-sized, chaos-causing Frog!

programme.desc: When Timmy sees a particularly grumpy farmer giving one of the flock a hard time, he decides to give him an idea of what it's like to be a sheep. But when the time comes to have the old farmer back, things aren't quite so simple.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When the Baron speeds up the rotation of Earth using windmills, Danger Mouse and Penfold age 300 years and must take on their nemesis as very, very, very old men.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes are relieved that the loud, threatening voice they can hear actually belongs to a small slug named Zorgoth, who is armed with a megaphone. However, when Zorgoth uses his Orb of Compression to shrink the heroes down to the size of ants, their relief evaporates. They must now embark on a long and dangerous quest, through their picnic items and beyond, to warn the Council before Zorgoth shrinks the entire forest.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck becomes a very serious librarian and makes it his mission to maintain the peace and quiet - that is until Frog arrives and has other ideas.

programme.title: Duck and Frog

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts embark on the biggest adventure ever! The crew is caught off guard by a volcanic eruption in the Pacific, but this single emergency soon turns into a global disaster when eruptions are triggered all across the Ring of Fire – a chain of hundreds of land and undersea volcanoes encircling the Pacific Ocean. As the Octonauts scramble to rise to the challenge of their multipronged rescue mission, Tweak has one last trick up her sleeve: the Gup-Z – a giant, untested new mecha Gup that will take the whole crew to operate.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: A small clown gets left behind by the circus.

programme.title: OOglies
programme.desc: The OOglies slip, slide and splat all over the place. This fast-paced madcap, stop-motion animation uncovers the mischief everyday household objects get up to when no-one is looking. The potato racers are driving each other round the bend, the DIY doughnuts attempt to fix the bathroom, the cavemen mushrooms come face to face with some big, hairy mammoth pots, fearless Pete Parsnip takes on a ferocious foe, and Slippery Nana takes on a pizza-cutting unicycle in the talent show.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog have one simple job: press buttons in a lift - and they can’t even do that right. Can they get the mayor to the ground floor in time for a grand opening?

programme.title: Jumanji: The Next Level
programme.desc: (2019) Adventure comedy sequel with Dwayne Johnson, Jack Black, Kevin Hart and Karen Gillan. The old school pals enter the Jumanji video game again to save their friend.

programme.title: Where the Crawdads Sing
programme.desc: (2022) Daisy Edgar-Jones stars in director Olivia Newman's adaptation of Delia Owens' best-selling novel about a feral child who's accused of murder in America's Deep South. Did she do it?

programme.title: Bohemian Rhapsody
programme.desc: (2018) Rami Malek turns in an Oscar-winning performance as Freddie Mercury in this rollercoaster musical biopic of the late Queen frontman. Big tunes. Big drama. Bigger teeth.

programme.title: Cocoon
programme.desc: (2020) Dreamy coming-of-age drama. During a sizzling Berlin summer, a 14-year-old girl who keeps caterpillars in a jar meets a cool girl. Will they become more than just friends? In German/subs.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Anastasia
programme.desc: (1997) Animated drama. When the Russian royal family is murdered, the sole surviving child is separated from her grandmother. Years later, conmen try to exploit the reward for her return.

programme.title: Ron's Gone Wrong
programme.desc: (2021) Animated comedy with Zach Galifianakis, Olivia Colman and Rob Delaney. Barney just wants a smart robot friend like everyone else at school. But will a faulty unit do the trick?

programme.title: The Lost World
programme.desc: (1960) Fantasy adventure. Claude Rains plays a scientist who, convinced that dinosaurs still roam the earth, sets off into the Amazon to find them. Based on Sir Arthur Conan Doyle's novel.

programme.title: Cheaper by the Dozen 2
programme.desc: (2005) Comedy. On holiday, Steve Martin, Bonnie Hunt and their huge family develop a rivalry with Eugene Levy, Carmen Electra and their eight children.

programme.title: Fisherman's Friends: One and All
programme.desc: (2022) Sea-shanty-singing comedy sequel with James Purefoy. The band are on tour but it's not all plain sailing. The label want Jim to replace his dead dad and Leadville just ain't woke.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Ghost...
programme.desc: ...Protocol: (2011) Part four of the Tom Cruise action-thriller franchise. The Impossible Missions Force fight to clear their name, while also trying to avert nuclear war. Busy, busy, busy!

programme.title: S.W.A.T.
programme.desc: (2003) Action with Colin Farrell and Samuel L Jackson. A loose canon joins an elite force to escort a fugitive to chokey. But the baddie's put a reward out for help with his escape.

programme.title: The Craft: Legacy
programme.desc: (2020) Supernatural thriller and standalone sequel to 1996's The Craft. A teenager discovers that she has magical abilities. But they come with dangerous consequences. [S]

programme.title: Dora and the Lost City of Gold
programme.desc: (2019) Family adventure with Isabela Moner, Eva Longoria and Michael Peña. Everyone's favourite young explorer must track down her parents, who are missing in Peru.

programme.title: The Golden Voyage of Sinbad
programme.desc: (1973) Sinbad and his crew battle against a master magician - who's determined to reach the Fountain of Destiny - and his monsters, provided by special effects maestro Ray Harryhausen

programme.title: The Mark of Zorro
programme.desc: (1974) Frank Langella plays a Mexican nobleman who returns home to find officials bleeding the country dry. So he dons a black mask and cape and becomes the avenging Zorro. [AD,S]

programme.title: Romancing the Stone
programme.desc: (1984) Comedy action adventure starring Michael Douglas and Kathleen Turner. An adventurer helps a bestselling author to search for her sister, who's been kidnapped in Colombia. [AD,S]

programme.title: Dante's Peak

programme.title: Jules

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: US animated comedy series. Wheels and the Legman try to hunt down the perpetrators who destroyed Steve's secret swimming hole, and Klaus helps an ageing Principal Lewis reclaim his confidence.

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Sitcom about three desperate young men who reside in ancient Rome. Marcus's parents are visiting for the weekend and he is keen to keep them and Delphine as far apart as possible - as Stylax pursues a romance with Ursula, one of the Vestal Virgins who guard the city's sacred flame, Grumio finds some mouldy cheese under his bed and spots a business opportunity.

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection. [S]

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated
programme.desc: Animated fun with Scooby-Doo and the gang. A famous ska band has risen from the grave and their haunted music threatens to make everyone in Crystal Cove dance themselves to death... that is, unless Shaggy and Scooby can stop them.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated
programme.desc: Animated fun with Scooby-Doo and the gang. When a gluten demon begins consuming all the bread, pasta and tasty treats in Crystal Cove, Shaggy and Scooby must out-eat the demon before it's too late.

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson narrates this series about the most entertaining Guinness World Records and the characters who set them as captured by TV shows around the globe. [S] S2 Ep2

programme.title: Billie & Greg: The Family Diaries
programme.desc: Reality series. Life for the Shepherds is as hectic as ever as Margot is rushed to hospital after suffering another seizure, and the family take part in a charity bike ride as Billie and Greg come to blows over Margot's safety - the family later head on a Mediterranean cruise but may not even make it when they aren't allowed on their flight. As the whole family comes together to celebrate Grandad Chatty's sobriety, they reflect on the last ten years.

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: The show where people hope to find true love through their love of food - one lucky celebrity gets the chance to find romance as they enjoy three very special meals cooked for them by three very special blind dates. In this episode, Ibiza Weekender star David Potts is going to be given five menus, each of which has been put together by a potential blind date - the five men behind the menus have all come up with a three-course meal, but David will only be having dinner with three of them. David will choose his dates based on the menus that he most likes the look of, but who will he want to see for seconds when the dinners have been eaten and the dates are done?

programme.title: Stuart Little 2
programme.desc: Part-animated family adventure comedy film sequel featuring the character created by American author EB White. Stuart's mother is being overprotective of him after he narrowly escapes injury in a football game - at the same time, his big brother George has made a new friend called Will, so Stuart is feeing lonely. When Stuart rescues a canary called Margalo from a falcon, she moves in with the Littles, but when she is nowhere to be found one day, Stuart and Snowbell set out to find her.

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Stuart Little 2
programme.desc: Part-animated family adventure comedy film sequel featuring the character created by American author EB White. Stuart's mother is being overprotective of him after he narrowly escapes injury in a football game - at the same time, his big brother George has made a new friend called Will, so Stuart is feeing lonely. When Stuart rescues a canary called Margalo from a falcon, she moves in with the Littles, but when she is nowhere to be found one day, Stuart and Snowbell set out to find her.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revamp of the iconic game show where three pairs of shoppers go wild in the aisles as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a 3,000-pound prize - along with the classic Supermarket Sweep quizzes, trolley dashes and pastel jumpers, the revamped show features exciting new games, challenges and some famous faces.

programme.title: Wheel of Fortune
programme.desc: Graham Norton invites nurse Chris, HR advisor Laura and school administrator Samantha to spin the wheel, solve puzzles and avoid bankrupts for a possible £50,000! But is The Wheel in a giving kind of mood?

programme.title: Peter Pan
programme.desc: Fantasy adventure. Wendy Darling mesmerises her brothers in stifling Edwardian London every night with bedtime tales of swordplay, swashbuckling and the fearsome Captain Hook, but the children become the heroes of an even greater story when Peter Pan flies into their nursery one night and leads them over moonlit rooftops through a galaxy of stars and to the lush jungles of Neverland. Here the children join Peter and the Lost Boys in an exhilarating life... and an inevitable showdown with Hook.

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Peter Pan
programme.desc: Fantasy adventure. Wendy Darling mesmerises her brothers in stifling Edwardian London every night with bedtime tales of swordplay, swashbuckling and the fearsome Captain Hook, but the children become the heroes of an even greater story when Peter Pan flies into their nursery one night and leads them over moonlit rooftops through a galaxy of stars and to the lush jungles of Neverland. Here the children join Peter and the Lost Boys in an exhilarating life... and an inevitable showdown with Hook.

programme.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa
programme.desc: Animated adventure comedy sequel. New York zoo animals Alex the lion, Marty the zebra, Melman the giraffe and Gloria the hippo are still stranded on Madagascar - when they try to leave the island, they end up crash-landing into the wilderness of an African nature reserve instead. As the gang meet others of their own kind, Alex discovers that he is a prince of a lion pride but has trouble communicating with his newfound family - will Alex accept his new position and end up staying in Africa?

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa
programme.desc: Animated adventure comedy sequel. programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a special celebrity edition of the much-loved game show as Denise Van Outen, Charlie Condou of Coronation Street, and Jo Brand line up to say what they see as they compete to identify well-known phrases and win thousands of pounds for their chosen charities.

programme.title: Wheel of Fortune
programme.desc: Graham Norton invites novelty shop owner Jess, learning and development trainer Xhemal, and former GP Pip to spin the Wheel, solve puzzles and hopefully hold their nerve to win a possible 50,000 pounds.

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Have you ever wondered how clever you are? This is your chance to find out as comedy legend Lee Mack presents the ultimate leveller of a quiz show where the questions are all about logic and common sense and where the whole family can play together - 100 contestants begin every show, but only some of them will get near the chance to answer a question that only one per cent of the country can get right for up to 100,000 pounds.

programme.title: Fast & Furious: Hobbs & Shaw
programme.desc: Action-adventure spin-off featuring characters from the Fast and Furious movie franchise. When cyber-genetically enhanced anarchist Brixton gains control of an insidious bio-threat that could alter humanity forever - and gets the better of a brilliant and fearless rogue MI6 agent - sworn enemies US agent Luke Hobbs and British mercenary Deckard Shaw have to partner up to bring down the only man who might be more villainous than themselves.

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Fast & Furious: Hobbs & Shaw
programme.desc: Action-adventure spin-off featuring characters from the Fast and Furious movie franchise. programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The men go to a ranch in order to regain their sense of masculinity. Elsewhere, Stewie has an accident on a slide that leads him to think he's started having periods.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Anarchic animated sitcom about a dysfunctional family in a wacky Rhode Island town. The Griffins present their versions of three shows broadcast by the US cable network HBO - the unwilling targets of their homages are Game of Thrones, Succession, and Big Little Lies.

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: US animated comedy series. The Smiths return from a difficult and quarrelsome holiday, only to end up on a desert island with no memory of who they are and an obsession with an old advertisement for mixed nuts.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Animated comedy about Bob Belcher, who runs Bob's Burgers restaurant, and his family. Tina attends a house party with the goal of slow dancing later with Jimmy Jr and touching 'upper butts', but butt sweat from the fast-paced dancing that precedes it causes her to soak through her skirt and problems arise when the skirt gets caught in a dryer - meanwhile, Teddy uses some wooden pallets to build a bar and seating in the Belchers' basement that becomes a hit with Dalton and his influencer friends.

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Sitcom about three desperate young men who reside in ancient Rome. When the boys get a dose of culture after Grumio is street-cast to play the part of Cupid in an upcoming play, Marcus becomes his agent and uses his new connections to impress Delphine - meanwhile, Stylax understudies the lead actor so he can meet some theatre groupies.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated
programme.desc: Animated fun with Scooby-Doo and the gang. When a motorcycle-riding robber known as the Dandy Highwayman takes more than just the usual riches on offer, it's up to the team to put a stop to his roguish ways.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated
programme.desc: Animated fun with Scooby-Doo and the gang. Professor Pericles sends Fred into an apocalyptic future in order to learn the location of the Planispheric Disc, but something's not right here...

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: Animated adventure series following Shaggy, Scooby, Velma, Daphne and Fred as they travel around on what could be their final summer break together. Daphne brings the gang to Ireland to visit an ancient Blake family castle - as the gang becomes enchanted by the magical Emerald Isle, an angry rampaging giant makes them reconsider just how wonderful all the old myths and legends really are...

programme.title: Dress To Impress
programme.desc: Fashion entrepreneur Caz hopes to be impressed by the style choices of three single ladies. Will any of them shine?

programme.title: Sam & Billie: Sister Act
programme.desc: Sam and Billie are back in this series that reunites the sisters as they enter the next chapter of their lives - with the kids a bit older, it's time for them to focus more on themselves and take a bit of sister time out whilst maintaining the crazy juggle of motherhood, full-on careers and celebrity life. This episode sees the sisters go on their first-ever holiday together to sunny Dubai which coincides with Sam's ambitious brand launch, but Billie is struggling with her health amidst the parties and glamour and receives a shocking diagnosis while there - meanwhile, Sam attempts to host an incredible super yacht party with Amanda Holden but the whole event is in jeopardy... will they even be able to leave port?

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: The show where people hope to find true love through their love of food - one lucky celebrity gets the chance to find romance as they enjoy three very special meals cooked for them by three very special blind dates. In this episode, reality TV star Bobby Norris is going to be given five menus, each of which has been put together by a potential blind date - the five men behind the menus have all come up with a three-course meal, but Bobby will only be having dinner with three of them. Bobby will choose his dates based on the menus that he most likes the look of, but who will he want to see for seconds when the dinners have been eaten and the dates are done?

programme.title: WhatsApp Obsession: The Murder of Stephanie Hansen
programme.desc: On the 30th of December, 2022, 39-year-old Stephanie Hansen was brutally stabbed to death in her London home - her housemate and friend Sheldon Rodrigues initially appeared grief-stricken, yet behind his facade lay a chilling obsession. In the months before Stephanie's death, Rodrigues planted secret cameras and recording devices in their home and bombarded her with coercive WhatsApp messages - when Steph began a new relationship, his jealousy turned deadly. Through exclusive access to police bodycam footage, WhatsApp records and court evidence, this film reveals how the Metropolitan Police exposed Rodrigues' lies and uncovered the disturbing level of surveillance Steph was subjected to - featuring interviews with Steph's family, friends and the detectives who investigated the case, this powerful documentary unravels a story of obsession, control, and the ultimate betrayal.

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The final episode, originally broadcast four days before star Sid James's untimely death from a heart attack, of the classic '70s sitcom. Jean is sulking at Sid while Sally and Mike won't talk to him - when Sid finds out why, he decides to take himself off to the pub.

programme.title: George & Mildred
programme.desc: 70s sitcom. Mildred's dreams of a cheap holiday clash with George's political allegiances.

programme.title: Steptoe and Son Ride Again
programme.desc: The second feature-length spin-off from the classic sitcom. Things are bad at the Steptoes' Shepherd's Bush rag-and-bone business - so bad, in fact, that Harold, who is always on the lookout for ways to improve his lot, invests his father's life savings in a greyhound who is almost blind and can't see the hare. When this plot fails, Harold is convinced that Albert must die so he can collect the old man's life insurance, so he asks him to play along with his latest bright idea...

programme.title: Carry On Again Doctor
programme.desc: British comedy. When Dr Nookey is disgraced and sent to a remote island hospital in the South Seas, he stumbles upon a shaman's weight-reducing potion that is given to him by Gladstone Screwer, one of the members of staff at the hospital. Realising the potential of the potion, Dr Nookey returns to England with the aim of making a fortune, but other interested parties want to cash in on his good fortunes, and others still just want him brought down a peg or two.

programme.title: Carry On Matron
programme.desc: Classic British comedy with the Carry On team about a gang's plan to raid a maternity hospital to steal a gigantic stock of birth control pills. One of the gang gets the lie of the land by disguising himself as a nurse, but the hospital's hypochondriac head doctor - who begins to make amorous advances towards him - and a formidable matron prove constant obstacles to the heist.

programme.title: Carry On Abroad
programme.desc: Classic British comedy. A package holiday abroad turns into a hilarious adventure for some unlucky holidaymakers - staying at the shoddy Palace Hotel on the island of Elsbels, they are disrupted by bad plumbing, a restaurant filled with mosquitoes, and a dawn chorus of cement mixers. All of this is on top of several intermarital and extramarital complications and misunderstandings during the stay - will all be well, or will all be hell? Innuendos and gags abound in this very British romp.

programme.title: Carry On Dick
programme.desc: Bawdy British comedy. It is 1750 and King George has set up the Bow Street Runners to combat the increase in crime - many highwaymen are captured, but not Dick Turpin, the most notorious of all. While disguised as a rector, his maid, and an organ player respectively, Dick and his accomplices are terrorising the countryside around Upper Dencher. Sir Roger Daley is determined to catch them at all costs with the help of Captain Fancey and Sergeant Jock Strapp, but will they escape Roger's grasp?

programme.title: Carry On... Don't Lose Your Head
programme.desc: British comedy in which the Carry On team have an irreverent poke at the French Revolution. In 1789 Paris, two English noblemen called Sir Rodney Ffing and Lord Darcy take it upon themselves to rescue their French counterparts from the clutches of the revolting masses, Citizen Robespierre, and the notorious Madame Guillotine. The Black Fingernail and his sidekick prove elusive, but Camembert - the chief of the secret police - and his comrade Bidet are determined to track them down...

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When the body of a young man is found in a Second World War-era motor car full of exhaust fumes, it looks like suicide - however, Simon Bright was not alone as he was hit on the head before he died. Where is his girlfriend and local newcomer Laura Sharp, and what is her hold on the people of Morton Fendle, a village still overshadowed by the gloomy spectre of the war?

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: The very first episode of the detective drama series following two detectives who seemingly have nothing in common but are thrown together and forge a rumbustious partnership in Bath. Elderly woman Jane Thompson is receiving threatening phone calls and hearing noises in the middle of the night - a local landowner is keen to get his hands on her cottage and Nick at first suspects him, but events unfold within the investigation to bring another suspect into the spotlight. Elsewhere, a small kitten is found and the station is torn over whether to put it down or keep it as a pet.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Faith Alexander arrives in Midsomer Deverell to meet her long-lost uncle Rex Masters, but Rex has told everyone that Faith's entire family was wiped out in a plane crash and he has disappeared - thanks to Miles King, an old friend who does Rex's gardening, news spreads quickly as Rex's cook Eileen Carnack, cleaner Janet Bailey, and Janet's fiance Jeremy Thacker rush to his home at Stonecroft House. Faith is anxious and can't understand how Rex has photographs of her when they haven't met following a 40-year family feud - after Barnaby asks Jones to investigate the disappearance, Rex's body is found in a river below Crofter's Weir. As Barnaby returns to Rex's study and finds some important clues in a set of risque photographs from the 1960s, can he get to the bottom of the tangled relationships stretching across the generations, learn the truth about Rex's life of mischief-making, and identify the killer?

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Detective drama series following two detectives who seemingly have nothing in common but are thrown together and forge a rumbustious partnership in Bath. Five friends take a hot air balloon trip but only four come back down alive, leaving McDonald and Dodds to untangle a complex web of lies spanning 30 years.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a maintenance engineer is found dead in the basement of the Bodleian Library, Lewis and Hathaway investigate a link between the murder and an intruder at a local professor's home earlier in the night - suspicion grows when they visit the engineer's house and discover rare 19th-century books which were taken from the library that he worked in.

programme.title: Lost Car Rescue
programme.desc: Reality series following an ambitious team of classic car hunters as they delve deep into Canada's northern wilderness to recover rare vehicles rumoured to be hiding in the bush. In this episode, Matt follows a childhood dream to track down the car owned by Al Capone, possibly America's most famous mobster, during his alleged Canadian hideaways.

programme.title: The Last Sunset
programme.desc: Western drama. Brendan O'Malley arrives at the Mexican home of old flame Belle Breckenridge to find her married to a drunkard getting ready for a cattle drive to Texas - hot on O'Malley's heels is lawman Dana Stribling, who has a personal reason for getting him back into his jurisdiction. Both men join Breckenridge and his wife on the drive, but tensions mount as they near Texas, not least because Stribling is starting to court Belle... and O'Malley is increasingly drawn to her daughter Missy.

programme.title: The Grand Fishing Adventure
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi is joined by angling buddy Roo Abbott and diver Luc Boerrigter for a mission where they must catch a carp with the bite filmed on underwater cameras - the destination is Chase Lakes in France, a 12-acre gravel pit with 250 carp weighing as much as over 70 pounds, but the carp are playing hard to get in the tough spring conditions and constant tweaking of tactics is required after seeing how they are feeding.

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole explores more UK rides and gives more tired bikes some much-needed attention in his workshop with the help of Allen Millyard. In this episode, Henry's Mediterranean odyssey continues with a ride through southern Spain - elsewhere, the restoration of the 1970 Chopper gathers pace, and Henry also gets a sneak peek at the latest models from AJS.

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Have you ever wondered how clever you are? Do you think you might be in the top 50 per cent of the country, the top ten per cent, or even the top one per cent? This is your chance to find out as Lee Mack hosts another episode in the second series of the show. The 1 Club is the ultimate leveller - it doesn't matter if you're not usually good at quizzes, because the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts... they're all about logic and common sense. It doesn't matter if you're 15 or 50 - this is the quiz that the whole family can play together. 100 contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question that only one per cent of the country can get right... and win up to 100,000 pounds?

programme.title: Rocky II
programme.desc: Boxing drama sequel. Although working-class Philadelphia boxer Rocky Balboa lost his high-profile bout with the cocky world champion Apollo Creed, his Cinderella story caught the national sports media's attention and has now given him the chance to capitalise on his sudden fame - meanwhile, Creed is still smarting from nearly losing to some fighter that no-one has ever heard of and so arrogantly prods his newfound nemesis into getting back into the ring.

Bond and Octopussy share a passionate attraction, but 007 soon discovers that the elegant exiled Afghan prince Kamal Khan is working with a mad Soviet officer to hurl mankind into World War III. As Bond tries to stop the nightmarish scheme, his exploits include a tense chase through the streets of India, a deadly brawl on top of a speeding train, and a treacherous mid-air knife fight on an airplane wing.
